title: Crawl Graphs, Frontier Strategy, Visited State, Depth, and Determinism · Code Recall
summary: A delayed crawl review reconstructs crawl graphs, frontier strategy, visited state, depth, and determinism from authorization, revision, seed, URL key, response identity, robots decision, parse record, extraction provenance, frontier event, report row, failure, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for deterministic crawl-frontier state machine after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggi…
objectives: Represent pages, fetch identities, redirect edges, discovery edges, canonical hints, embedded resources, and rejection states separately.
Choose FIFO, depth-first, priority, sitemap, recrawl, or hybrid ordering from stakeholder value and fairness requirements.
Reserve an admitted URL atomically before scheduling and reconcile queued, active, completed, redirected, rejected, and retry states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

stimulus.title: Deterministic Frontier Evidence case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review site-quality team handles case 5970 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile crawl graph, atomic scheduling, frontier state, parents, depth, and deterministic order; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use stable tie-breakers and seeded fixtures so the same admitted graph yields a reproducible decision and report order.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Deterministic tests require disabling concurrency entirely.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use stable tie-breakers and seeded fixtures so the same admitted graph yields a reproducible decision and report order. Artifact under review: deterministic crawl-frontier state machine.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Deterministic Frontier Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use stable tie-breakers and seeded fixtures so the same admitted graph yields a reproducible decision and report order.
